questions.q: Garuda is the divine mount (vahana) of which great god?
questions.explanation: Garuda is the celestial eagle who serves as the mount of Vishnu.

questions.q: What kind of creature is Garuda?
questions.explanation: Garuda is depicted as a great eagle, the king of birds.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Garuda comes to free Rama and Lakshmana from bonds made of what?
questions.explanation: Garuda freed the brothers from the serpent-arrows that had coiled around them.

questions.q: Garuda is the natural enemy of which kind of beings?
questions.explanation: Garuda is famed as the foe of snakes (nagas), which is why his approach scattered the serpent-bonds.

questions.q: Which two brothers did Garuda revive after they fell bound on the battlefield?
questions.explanation: Garuda freed and revived Rama and Lakshmana, who had been struck down by serpent-arrows.

questions.q: The serpent-arrows that bound Rama and Lakshmana were fired by which warrior?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, son of Ravana, used the nagapasha (serpent-noose) weapon to bind the brothers.

questions.q: What effect did Garuda's mere approach have on the serpents binding the brothers?
questions.explanation: At the approach of Garuda, their natural enemy, the serpents fled and the bonds dissolved.

questions.q: Garuda is the king of which group of creatures?
questions.explanation: Garuda is honored as the king of birds in Hindu tradition.

questions.q: When Garuda departed after healing the brothers, how did Rama feel toward him?
questions.explanation: Rama was filled with gratitude and wonder at the eagle who had saved them.

questions.q: Garuda's wings are most often described as having what quality?
questions.explanation: Garuda is traditionally described with golden, radiant wings befitting Vishnu's mount.

questions.q: Who was the father of Garuda according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Garuda was born to the sage Kashyapa and his wife Vinata.

questions.q: Who was the mother of Garuda?
questions.explanation: Garuda's mother was Vinata, while the serpents were born of her co-wife Kadru.

questions.q: The serpents (nagas), Garuda's eternal foes, were born of which mother?
questions.explanation: The serpents were the offspring of Kadru, making them cousins and rivals of Garuda.

questions.q: The serpent-weapon Indrajit used to bind the brothers is best known by what name?
questions.explanation: The nagapasha was the serpent-noose weapon that ensnared Rama and Lakshmana.

questions.q: In the famous tale of his birth, Garuda obtained which substance to free his mother from servitude?
questions.explanation: Garuda seized the amrita (nectar of immortality) from the gods to ransom his enslaved mother Vinata.

questions.q: Garuda's mother Vinata was held in servitude because of a wager lost over the color of what?
questions.explanation: Vinata lost a bet with Kadru over the color of the divine horse Uchchaihshravas's tail.

questions.q: When Garuda came to free the brothers, the battle had taken place near which city?
questions.explanation: The serpent-bond episode occurred during the war at Lanka against Ravana's forces.

questions.q: After being revived by Garuda, Rama and Lakshmana regained which qualities at once?
questions.explanation: Garuda's touch restored the brothers' strength, vigor, and even enhanced their splendor.

questions.q: Which of these is a well-known alternate name or epithet for Garuda?
questions.explanation: Garuda is often called Suparna, meaning 'the well-winged one.'

questions.q: Garuda is frequently portrayed in art as having a body that is part man and part what?
questions.explanation: Garuda is commonly depicted with a human torso and the wings, beak, and talons of a great bird.

questions.q: Garuda's role as Vishnu's mount links him most closely to which of these in the Ramayana?
options: Rama as an incarnation of Vishnu
questions.explanation: Because Rama is an avatar of Vishnu, Garuda, Vishnu's eagle, aids him in his hour of need.

questions.q: What is the chief emotion the watching armies felt when Garuda descended to free the brothers?
questions.explanation: The hosts watched in wonder and awe as the radiant eagle scattered the serpents and healed Rama and Lakshmana.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, before Garuda's arrival, what state were Rama and Lakshmana in?
questions.explanation: Bound by the nagapasha, the brothers had fallen and lay senseless until Garuda revived them.

questions.q: According to the narrative, what did Garuda do to the brothers' wounds besides loosening the bonds?
options: He stroked their faces, healing every wound
He carried them to the Himalayas
questions.explanation: Garuda gently stroked the brothers' faces, and their wounds healed and their bodies grew radiant.

questions.q: In the larger Puranic tradition, Garuda's intense enmity with serpents is rooted in the enslavement of whom?
questions.explanation: The serpents' mother Kadru enslaved Garuda's mother Vinata, fueling Garuda's lifelong hatred of snakes.

questions.q: Garuda's elder brother, born prematurely and lacking fully formed legs, became the charioteer of which deity?
questions.explanation: Aruna, Garuda's brother, became the charioteer of Surya, the sun god.

questions.q: When Garuda asks Rama who he is, the episode emphasizes that even Garuda did not at first fully grasp what about Rama?
questions.explanation: The encounter underscores Rama's hidden divinity as Vishnu, of whom Garuda is the eternal mount.

questions.q: After healing the brothers in some retellings, Garuda offers Rama which kind of parting advice or blessing?
options: To remain ever vigilant and victorious
To return to Ayodhya at once
questions.explanation: Garuda blesses Rama to remain vigilant and assures him of ultimate victory before flying away.

questions.q: Garuda's swiftness in the tradition is so great that he is said to be able to outpace what?
questions.explanation: Garuda is praised as swifter than the wind and as fast as thought itself.

questions.q: The serpent-bond episode that Garuda resolves is associated with which of Indrajit's tactics in the war?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, a master of illusion, struck the brothers with the serpent-arrows while hidden from view.
